description: DETERMINISM NEGATIVE of engine-on-timeout: identical source + a 1000ms <onTimeout>, but the input drives ONLY {"wait":"settle"} — NO advance-time. Because ms>0 timers fire EXCLUSIVELY on the virtual clock's advance-time verb (never on a wall clock, never via settle's macrotask hop), the timer does NOT fire: @light stays .Green. This is the load-bearing proof that the virtual clock is deterministic — a settle alone can never advance time.
rationale: The conformance virtual clock advances ONLY on advance-time. settle() does a microtask + a setTimeout(0) macrotask hop (which passes through to the real timer for task-queue ordering) but never advances virtual time, so a 1000ms onTimeout cannot fire without an explicit advance-time. Pairs with engine-on-timeout to bracket the verb: with it the timer fires, without it it does not.
